content.verdict: Rosewood Le Guanahani is St Barths' grand-resort answer to the island's small-villa-hotel norm — 67 brightly painted Caribbean cottages spread across an entire 16-acre peninsula at Anse de Grand Cul-de-Sac, with two private beaches, three restaurants, and the kind of in-house breadth that gives a honeymoon real range. Rosewood took over the property in 2021 with a top-to-bottom renovation by the Paris-based studio of Luis Pons, who restored the signature pink, lavender, and turquoise gingerbread cottages of the original 1980s Guanahani while updating every interior to a relaxed contemporary-Creole standard. The result is the rare St Barths resort where you can spend a full week without a car: the calm lagoon side is perfect for kite-surfing and paddleboarding, the Atlantic side has the dramatic surf and sunrise views, and the three restaurants — Beach House, Lounge, and Wave — cover the full day from breakfast through late dinner. For honeymooners who want the full glamour of St Barths combined with a real resort's amenities (spa, kids' club for future trips, tennis, two pools, multiple bars), it is the most complete property on the island and the strongest value at the top end given how much is included.
content.best_room: The Pointe Suite is the signature category — a one-bedroom standalone cottage at the very tip of the peninsula with 270-degree water views, private terrace and plunge pool, and complete seclusion from the main resort traffic. For pure beach proximity, the Beachfront Garden Cottages on the lagoon side put you 20 metres from the water with a private outdoor shower and sun deck. Avoid the Hillside Garden View category for a honeymoon — the views and breeze make a real difference here.

itinerary_7_nights.title: St Jean Landing & Peninsula Arrival
itinerary_7_nights.description: Your Winair Twin Otter approach over the St Jean hilltop is St Barths' famous arrival — short, steep, and over within 15 seconds of clearing the ridge. Rosewood's car collects you for the 10-minute drive to the Grand Cul-de-Sac peninsula. Take the welcome rum punch at the Lounge, settle into your cottage, and have a quiet first dinner at Beach House on the lagoon side.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Two-Beach Tour
itinerary_7_nights.description: Rosewood's setting on a peninsula gives you two completely different beaches without leaving the property. The lagoon side is calm, shallow, and the island's best kite-surfing and paddleboard water. The Atlantic side has dramatic surf and the sunrise view. Spend the day moving between them, take a couples massage at the Sense Spa in the late afternoon, and dinner at Wave for the chef's tasting menu.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Gustavia Harbour & Shell Beach
itinerary_7_nights.description: Drive across the island (15 minutes) to Gustavia, the Swedish colonial capital and yacht-set centre. Lunch harbour-front at Bonito or L'Isola, then walk to Shell Beach for the afternoon — the central-Gustavia beach made of crushed coral and famous for its sunset bar Shellona. Return to Rosewood for the lagoon at golden hour.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Saline & Gouverneur
itinerary_7_nights.description: The two most beautiful beaches on St Barths sit on the south coast: Saline, a wild crescent with no buildings or beach service, and Gouverneur, slightly more accessible but equally cinematic. Pack a picnic from the Rosewood beach team and spend the day moving between them. Stop at L'Esprit Saline or Maya's for a long, late lunch on the way back.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Catamaran to Île Fourchue
itinerary_7_nights.description: The concierge will arrange a private or semi-private catamaran day to Île Fourchue, the uninhabited bird-sanctuary island 30 minutes north of St Barths. Snorkelling here is exceptional — turtles, eagle rays, and pristine reef. Return for a long sunset on the Atlantic side of the peninsula and a quiet dinner at Beach House.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Anse des Cayes Surfing & Colombier Walk
itinerary_7_nights.description: Anse des Cayes on the north coast is the island's only consistent surf break — Rosewood will arrange a lesson with a local instructor. After lunch, walk down to Anse de Colombier from Petite Anse — 20 minutes through scrub to a road-free beach with no buildings, and the most peaceful crescent on the island. Sunset cocktails at Nikki Beach St Barths or back at the Rosewood Lounge.

itinerary_7_nights.title: Last Lagoon Morning
itinerary_7_nights.description: A slow breakfast at your cottage, a final swim on whichever beach you have decided you prefer, and the same thrilling 10-seater Twin Otter departure that opened the trip. Rosewood arranges the airport transfer so you arrive 30 minutes before the inter-island hop to St Martin.

caveats: St Barths is one of the most expensive islands in the Caribbean — flights via St Martin add several hundred dollars per person, and on-island restaurant prices regularly exceed Paris or New York. Budget realistically.

The Gustaf III Airport approach over the St Jean hilltop is genuinely steep and the runway is one of the world's shortest — completely safe with experienced Winair pilots, but worth knowing in advance for nervous flyers.
Hurricane season runs August to October, with September the highest-risk month. The Rosewood typically reduces operations in September; book December through July for the most reliable weather.

The peninsula is a 12-minute drive from Gustavia restaurants — most guests rent a Mini Moke or open Jeep for the week to access the south-coast beaches and the Gustavia dining scene without relying on hotel cars.

faqs.question: Is Rosewood Le Guanahani good for honeymooners?
faqs.answer: It is the most complete resort on St Barths — two beaches, three restaurants, a serious spa, and the standalone-cottage layout means real privacy. The mix of full-resort amenities and Rosewood's signature soft-luxury service makes it ideal for couples who want range without leaving the property each day.

faqs.question: What is the best beach for a honeymoon on St Barths?
faqs.answer: On the resort, the Atlantic side of the Rosewood peninsula is the most dramatic and least busy. Off-property, Saline and Gouverneur on the south coast are the island's iconic crescents, and Anse de Colombier is the walk-only secret. Skip St Jean if you want privacy — it is busy and built up.

faqs.question: How do we get to St Barths from the US?
faqs.answer: There are no direct flights from the US — you connect via St Martin (Princess Juliana SXM) or San Juan, Puerto Rico (SJU). From SXM, Winair operates 10-seater Twin Otters to Gustaf III several times daily (10 minutes). A fast catamaran ferry from Marigot is the alternative (45 minutes).

faqs.question: When is the best time to visit?
faqs.answer: December through April is the peak Caribbean season — ideal weather, calm seas, and the island's most vibrant atmosphere. January and February are the absolute peak. Late April to early July offers lower rates with generally excellent weather. Avoid August through October for hurricane risk.

faqs.question: What's included in the rate?
faqs.answer: Breakfast at the Lounge, beach service on both beaches (loungers, towels, umbrellas, F&B), use of non-motorised watersports (paddleboards, kayaks, snorkelling gear), the fitness centre, and tennis court are all included. Spa treatments, motorised watersports, restaurant dinners, and private charters are charged separately.

faqs.question: Is Rosewood Le Guanahani adults-only?
faqs.answer: No — Rosewood operates a small kids' club and welcomes families, particularly during US school holidays. Outside those weeks the atmosphere is overwhelmingly couples and adult groups, and the standalone-cottage layout means a honeymoon stay rarely intersects with family activity.
